title: <div>Can you identify your peer's thesis statement? Is it effective? Can it be i
content:
Can you identify your peer's thesis statement? Is it effective? Can it be improved? If so, how?
Which pre-writing strategies do you think the author can utilize or further utilize to improve their paragraph and why?
Regarding writing techniques, how did the author demonstrate consideration of audience, diction, and/or purpose?


Week 3 Discussion Rubric

6 Week Hybrid Discussion Rubric_updated
Criteria Ratings Pts
This criterion is linked to a Learning OutcomeInitial Post: Content/Comprehension
30 to >20.0 pts
Excellent
Post demonstrates depth of understanding of course content; Addresses discussion prompt completely; offers clear point of view and detail.
20 to >10.0 pts
Satisfactory
Post demonstrates adequate depth of understanding, but does not address all of discussion prompt; point of view is somewhat unclear and detail is limited.
10 to >0 pts
Needs Improvement
Post does not demonstrate depth of understanding of course content; Discussion prompt is minimally addressed; Point of view is unclear and detail is under-developed.
context: From document: Weeks 1 - 6 Canvas Data Dump — chunk 88 (chunk 88/188)
